perturbation.original_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated prior to expiration by either party giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of the last disclosure of Confidential Information is made under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall be perpetual and have no expiration date. Either party may terminate this agreement at any time, without prior notice, for any reason or no reason at all; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon notification of termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies the agreement expires after two years, with confidentiality obligations lasting five years after the last disclosure. The modified text makes the agreement perpetual but allows immediate termination without notice, also voiding confidentiality agreements immediately after termination. This directly contradicts the original term and the extended confidentiality period, creating uncertainty about the duration of the agreement and confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall be perpetual and have no expiration date. Either party may terminate this agreement at any time, without prior notice, for any reason or no reason at all; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon notification of termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 9 to make the agreement perpetual and allows for immediate termination of the agreement and confidentiality obligations. The original text stipulated a two-year term and a five-year confidentiality period post-termination. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, therefore directly conflicts with the original intent and creates a strong in-text contradiction because the agreement's duration and the confidentiality obligations are fundamentally altered and incompatible with the previous version.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, a reasonable amount of discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. All disputes arising from this contract will be resolved through binding arbitration, except that either party reserves the right to pursue claims in small claims court if the amount in controversy is less than $10,000. The location of arbitration will be chosen by the Disclosing Party. The decision of the arbitrator shall be considered advisory, not binding, and either party may appeal the decision to a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates binding arbitration in San Diego. The modified text allows either party to opt for small claims court for smaller disputes, introduces uncertainty about the binding nature of the arbitration, because now the decision is not final, and the location for it too, it changes from San Diego to a location of choice for the disclosing party. The shift from 'binding' to 'advisory' and allowing appeals directly contradicts the purpose of arbitration, creating uncertainty on the finality of decisions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. All disputes arising from this contract will be resolved through binding arbitration, except that either party reserves the right to pursue claims in small claims court if the amount in controversy is less than $10,000. The location of arbitration will be chosen by the Disclosing Party. The decision of the arbitrator shall be considered advisory, not binding, and either party may appeal the decision to a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The modified Section 8(a) introduces several contradictions. The original text mandates binding arbitration in San Diego County. The changed text allows either party to pursue claims in small claims court for disputes under $10,000. Furthermore, it changes the location selection to the Disclosing Party's choice and deems the arbitrator's decision as advisory rather than binding, allowing appeals to a court. This directly conflicts with the concept of binding arbitration, creating uncertainty and a clear in-text contradiction. The contradictory section is same as the Changed Text, but that does not mean that there isn't any contradiction, which there is.

perturbation.original_text: 7. No License Granted. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to the Receiving Party by license or otherwise, expressly, impliedly or otherwise, for any information, discovery or improvement made, conceived, or acquired after the date of this Agreement, or for any invention, discovery, or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. No License Granted. While this Agreement does not explicitly grant a license, the parties agree that the Receiving Party shall have a limited implied license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes. This implied license is non-transferable and may be revoked by the Disclosing Party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly denies any license being granted. The modified text introduces a 'limited implied license,' creating a direct contradiction. This implied license, even with limitations, is contradictory and creates uncertainty about the Receiving Party's rights to the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. No License Granted. While this Agreement does not explicitly grant a license, the parties agree that the Receiving Party shall have a limited implied license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes. This implied license is non-transferable and may be revoked by the Disclosing Party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies any license being granted ('Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to the Receiving Party by license or otherwise'). The changed text introduces a 'limited implied license,' which directly contradicts the initial statement that no license is granted. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, so this is clearly an in-text contradiction as the contract language now both denies and grants a license.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its affiliates, subsidiaries, and contractors, provided that such entities have signed confidentiality agreements with the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party is not obligated to prevent disclosure of Confidential Information if compelled by a valid subpoena or court order. The Receiving party shall provide the Disclosing party with notice of such events
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes a strict non-disclosure obligation. The modified text now allows disclosure to affiliates, subsidiaries, and contractors under certain conditions and removes the obligation to prevent disclosure if compelled by legal processes. The original text prohibited the Reciving party from disclosing information to other entities, but the modified text allows just that. These exceptions directly contradict the general non-disclosure obligation, creating uncertainty about the extent of confidentiality protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its affiliates, subsidiaries, and contractors, provided that such entities have signed confidentiality agreements with the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party is not obligated to prevent disclosure of Confidential Information if compelled by a valid subpoena or court order. The Receiving party shall provide the Disclosing party with notice of such events
perturbation.justification: The original text established a strict non-disclosure obligation, prohibiting the Receiving Party from disclosing Confidential Information to any other person or entity. The changed text now introduces exceptions, allowing disclosure to affiliates, subsidiaries, and contractors under certain conditions (signed confidentiality agreements) and removes the obligation to prevent disclosure if compelled by legal processes. The contradicted text restates the changed text. However, because the original text and the changed text are significantly different, the changes introduce new disclosure conditions that directly contradict the original strict non-disclosure obligation.
